title: notifications-mail — compliance evidence
description: Primary control point for CAN-SPAM (postal address footer + one-click unsubscribe), CASL (express consent tracking for CA), and GDPR Art. 6/7/21 (mail-specific enforcement).

controls.evidence: MJML layout injects workspace sender identity in every email. From address matches workspace.sender_identity.
Subject rendered from published template; template review workflow (versioning + HasVersions) provides trail.
MJML footer injects workspace.postal_address from settings.json. Marketing sends refused when strict_can_spam_footer flag on + address missing.
One-click unsubscribe via signed URL (List-Unsubscribe-Post header per RFC 8058). PreferenceUpdated event fires immediately — opt-out effective within seconds, far exceeding 10 days.

casl.note: Applies to Canadian recipients. Detected via user.country_code = CA.

controls.evidence: Marketing-priority sends refused for CA recipients unless express consent captured via compliance module. Consent trail auditable via HasAuditable on NotificationPreference.
marketing_opt_in categories require enabled=true on NotificationPreference. Bulk send guards check per-recipient consent.
Unsubscribe via mail footer OR preferences page. Processed within seconds — immediate compliance.
subprocessors.json lists Mailgun / SendGrid / SES / Postmark / Resend as Article 28 processors. DPAs on file, referenced by DSAR orchestrator.
TLS 1.3 for outbound; provider webhooks signature-verified inbound; secrets in Doppler.
Every mail carries `List-Unsubscribe: <mailto>, <https>` + `List-Unsubscribe-Post: List-Unsubscribe=One-Click` headers. Gmail / Yahoo / Apple Mail honour one-click unsubscribe button natively.
MJML layout validated against Litmus contrast checks in CI.
MJML linter refuses templates with <mj-image> missing alt.
feature-flags.json per-provider kill switches. Emergency ops can flip provider off in seconds.
Template + suppression HasVersions snapshots + audit trail.
